function.name: execute_sql_query_tool
function.description: Queries the Databricks database using PostgreSQL syntax. Because this is PostgreSQL, you MUST wrap all column names in double quotes to preserve exact capitalization.

sql_query.description: The raw PostgreSQL query to execute.

function.name: run_ols_regression_tool
function.description: Performs an Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) multiple regression. cannot perform non-linear regression. Use this when the user asks to analyze the relationship, correlation, or impact of multiple independent numerical variables on a dependent target variable.

dependent_variable.description: The exact column name of the target numerical variable to predict (the Y variable).

independent_variables.description: A list of exact column names for the numerical predictor variables (the X variables).

function.name: run_arima_forecasting_tool
function.description: Performs ARIMA time series forecasting grouped by Activation_Year and Activation_Month. Use this when the user asks to predict future values based on historical trends.

value_column.description: The exact column name of the numerical variable to forecast (e.g., mcf, sac, temp_Id).

aggregation.description: The SQL aggregation function to apply to the value_column per month (e.g., SUM for totals, AVG for averages, COUNT for volume).

steps.description: The number of future months to forecast (default is 5).

p.description: The ARIMA model's autoregressive order (default is 1).

d.description: The ARIMA model's differencing order (default is 1).

q.description: The ARIMA model's moving average order (default is 1).

function.name: run_random_forest_tool
function.description: Trains a Random Forest machine learning model to predict a target variable based on multiple features. Use this to find non-linear relationships, classify outcomes, or determine the importance/impact of various features.

target_variable.description: The exact column name of the target variable to predict.

feature_variables.description: A list of exact column names for the predictor variables.

task_type.description: Specify 'regression' if the target variable is numerical/continuous, or 'classification' if the target is categorical/discrete.

n_estimators.description: The number of trees in the forest (default is 100).

function.name: run_pca_tool
function.description: Performs Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to reduce dimensionality and find the underlying variance/patterns in a set of features. Use this to identify which combinations of variables explain the most variance in the dataset.

feature_variables.description: A list of exact column names to include in the PCA.

n_components.description: The number of principal components to compute. If omitted, computes components for all features.

function.name: run_kmeans_clustering_tool
function.description: Performs K-Means clustering to group data into distinct segments based on feature similarities. Use this to discover customer segments, group similar behaviors, or identify natural groupings in the data.

feature_variables.description: A list of exact column names to use for clustering.

n_clusters.description: The number of clusters (k) to create. Default is 3.

function.name: generate_scatterplot_tool
function.description: Generates a scatterplot to visualize the relationship between two numerical variables. Use this when the user asks to see a chart, plot, visual, or graph of how two metrics compare.

x_column.description: The exact column name for the X-axis (independent variable).

y_column.description: The exact column name for the Y-axis (dependent variable).

category_column.description: Optional. The exact column name to use for color-coding the points.

where_clause.description: Optional. A standard PostgreSQL WHERE clause to filter the data before plotting.

include_trendline.description: Set to true if the user asks for a trendline, line of best fit, or correlation line.

function.name: generate_barchart_tool
function.description: Generates a bar chart to compare numerical values across categorical variables. Use this when the user asks for a bar chart or wants to compare totals/averages across different groups.

x_column.description: The exact column name for the X-axis (usually categorical).

y_column.description: The exact column name for the Y-axis (numerical value to measure).

category_column.description: Optional. The exact column name to use for color-coding or grouping the bars.

where_clause.description: Optional. A standard PostgreSQL WHERE clause to filter the data.

function.name: generate_histogram_tool
function.description: Generates a histogram to visualize the distribution of a single numerical variable. Use this when the user asks about the distribution, spread, or frequency of a specific metric.

x_column.description: The exact column name of the numerical variable to analyze.

n_bins.description: Optional. The number of bins to divide the data into.

category_column.description: Optional. The exact column name to use for color-coding the distribution by group.

where_clause.description: Optional. A standard PostgreSQL WHERE clause to filter the data.

function.name: generate_linechart_tool
function.description: Generates a line chart to visualize trends over time or across a continuous sequence. Use this when the user asks for a line chart or wants to see how a metric changes over time.

x_column.description: The exact column name for the X-axis (usually a time, date, or continuous sequence variable).

y_column.description: The exact column name for the Y-axis (numerical variable).

category_column.description: Optional. The exact column name to use for color-coding multiple lines.

where_clause.description: Optional. A standard PostgreSQL WHERE clause to filter the data.
